channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: The Latest: S&P 500 falls 2% at the open despite Trump’s tariff pause
item.description: U.S. stocks are giving back some of their historic gains from the day before as Wall Street weighs a global trade war that has cooled in temperature but is still threatening the economy.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Author Jillian Lauren, wife of Weezer bassist, arrested after being shot by police
item.description: Jillian Lauren, author and wife of Weezer bassist Scott Shriner, was shot and injured by Los Angeles police and arrested on suspicion of attempted murder after pointing a gun at officers from her front yard, authorities said.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Billionaires, trusted allies, media personalities. These are the people Trump picked for top roles
item.description: President Donald Trump prioritized loyalty as he built out a team for his second term, surrounding himself with people who served faithfully in his first administration or who worked on one or more of his three campaigns.
item.dc:creator: PARKER KAUFMANN, HUMERA LODHI and CHRISTOPHER KELLER, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Shark attack survivor presses state for alert system to keep people safe in the water
item.description: An Alabama teen who narrowly survived a fierce shark attack last year said she hopes a proposed alert system before state lawmakers can help keep others safe in the water.
item.dc:creator: KIM CHANDLER Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Countries will follow Trump imposing tariffs on China: Gordon Chang
item.description: Chang, author of the new book "Plan Red: China's Project to Destroy America," said he expects to see tariffs rise on Chinese goods worldwide.
item.dc:creator: Damita Menezes, NewsNation

item.title: Bahamas officials reveal American man's cause of death after body found on beach
item.description: Bahamas police have revealed the cause of death for an American man who briefly disappeared during a vacation with his parents and was found dead the next morning.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Sunny and pleasant afternoon, with warmer temperatures to come
item.description: SALT LAKE CITY (ABC4) - Happy Wednesday, Utah! High pressure is slowly building in from the west and taking over our weather pattern. The high pressure will not only bring dry weather to the forecast but also come with the return of even warmer temperatures.
item.dc:creator: Alana Brophy, ABC 4

item.title: Dolphins player Tyreek Hill and his wife were involved in a domestic dispute, police say
item.description: SUNNY ISLES BEACH, Fla. (AP) — Miami Dolphins wide receiver Tyreek Hill and his wife were involved in a domestic dispute at their South Florida high-rise condominium, but no charges were filed, according to a police report.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Vice president of Bikers Against Child Abuse chapter arrested for sex crimes against children
item.description: New York State Police said the vice president of Buffalo's Bikers Against Child Abuse chapter has been arrested for sex crimes against children.
item.dc:creator: Kayleigh Hunter-Gasperini, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Pennsylvania judge is convicted of shooting and wounding her ex-boyfriend as he slept
item.description: HARRISBURG, Pa. (AP) — A magistrate judge in Pennsylvania accused of shooting and wounding her ex-boyfriend in the head as he slept was convicted Wednesday of attempted homicide and aggravated assault charges.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Florida man arrested after surveillance footage captures daylight road rage shootout
item.description: Jesus Manuel Perez is charged with attempted murder and a host of other crimes after the March 30 road rage incident.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Universal Studios announces plans to build first European theme park resort
item.description: Universal Destinations & Experiences officially announced on Tuesday that it intends to build its first-ever theme park and resort in the United Kingdom. In 2023, Universal purchased a 476-acre parcel of land outside Bedford, England, about 55 miles north of London.
item.dc:creator: Iman Palm, KTLA 5

item.title: Previous execution reveals how South Carolina will put 2nd inmate to death by firing squad
item.description: COLUMBIA, S.C. (AP) — South Carolina is legally required to keep some protocols secret as it prepares to put a man to death by firing squad for the murder of a public safety officer, but the only previous execution by bullets the state carried out last month reveals a few key details.
item.dc:creator: JEFFREY COLLINS, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Group calls for investigation into Ms. Rachel over posts about Gaza children
item.description: Rachel Griffin Accurso, who goes by Ms. Rachel to her more than 14 million subscribers on YouTube, makes videos for children.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, Fox 5

item.title: Watch: US trade representative testifies before House on Trump trade agenda
item.description: U.S. Trade Representative Jamieson Greer testified before the House Ways and Means Committee on Wednesday morning on President Trump's trade agenda, as the latest reciprocal tariffs took effect overnight.
item.dc:creator: The Hill Staff, NewsNation

item.title: Oklahoma woman accused of laundering $1.5M from elderly victims
item.description: Charges have been filed against Christine Echohawk for laundering about $1.5 million obtained through romance scams, officials said.
item.dc:creator: Caroline Sellers/KFOR, NewsNation

item.title: Wall Street surges to one of its best days since WWII after Trump pauses most tariffs
item.description: U.S. stocks soared to one of their best days in history on a euphoric Wall Street Wednesday after President Donald Trump said he would back off on most of his tariffs temporarily, as investors had so desperately hoped he would.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Rich on Tech Radio Recap: Nintendo News, Mesh Wi-Fi & Online Privacy
item.description: You see my segments on TV, but did you know I also host a national radio show called Rich On Tech? Each week, I share helpful tech news, tips, and answer your questions on radio stations across the country — from KFI AM 640 in Los Angeles to 710 WOR in New York City.
item.dc:creator: Rich DeMuro, KTLA 5

item.title: Wall Street surges to one of its best days since WWII after Trump pauses most tariffs
item.description: U.S. stocks soared to one of their best days in history on a euphoric Wall Street Wednesday after President Donald Trump said he would back off on most of his tariffs temporarily, as investors had so desperately hoped he would.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Trump to fine migrants up to $1K daily for defying deportation order
item.description: The administration is planning to retroactively apply the fines for up to five years, which means some immigrants could owe more than $1 million.
item.dc:creator: Tom Dempsey, WGN 9

item.title: Stowaways found dead in JetBlue landing gear identified as teens
item.description: Two stowaways found dead in the landing gear compartment of a JetBlue plane earlier this year have been identified as teenagers, according to media reports. The teens were discovered Jan.
item.dc:creator: Josh DuBose, KTLA 5

item.title: 96-year-old resident at southern Utah care home dies after being found in pool
item.description: A resident of a senior living facility in southern Utah has died after being found in the community's pool on Monday.
item.dc:creator: Trevor Myers, ABC 4

item.title: Hearing looks at rules for evidence and other details in Bryan Kohberger's quadruple murder trial
item.description: Prosecutors and attorneys for a man charged in the killings of four University of Idaho students in 2022 began arguing some of the final ground rules they want for Bryan Kohberger’s trial.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Boone, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Human remains found in 1973 identified as missing Kansas teen
item.description: Human remains found near Garnett, Kansas, in 1973 have been positively identified more than 50 years later.
item.dc:creator: Brian Dulle, NewsNation

item.title: Trump signs executive orders to boost coal, a reliable but polluting energy source
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Donald Trump on Tuesday signed a series of executive orders aimed at boosting the struggling coal industry, a reliable but polluting energy source that’s long been in decline.
item.dc:creator: MATTHEW DALY, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Human remains found in 1973 identified as missing Kansas teen
item.description: Human remains found near Garnett, Kansas, in 1973 have been positively identified more than 50 years later.
item.dc:creator: Brian Dulle, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Judge tells government to provide evidence, or case against Columbia student Mahmoud Khalil is over
item.description: JENA, La. (AP) — An immigration judge in Louisiana said she would terminate the case against Mahmoud Khalil if the government does not provide evidence this week justifying their attempted deportation of the Columbia University student activist.
item.dc:creator: SARA CLINE and JAKE OFFENHARTZ, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: A Florida man is executed for killing a Miami Herald employee who was abducted on her lunch break
item.description: STARKE, Fla. (AP) — A Florida man convicted of killing a Miami Herald employee who was abducted on her lunch break was executed Tuesday evening. Michael Tanzi was pronounced dead at 6:12 p.m.
item.dc:creator: CURT ANDERSON, Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Judge rules in favor of AP in 'Gulf of America' spat
item.description: A federal judge has ordered the Trump administration to restore the Associated Press's full media access, which was suspended after the news organization said it would not follow Trump's executive order to refer to the Gulf of Mexico as the Gulf of America.
item.dc:creator: Travis Schlepp, KTLA 5

item.title: Keystone oil pipeline shutdown could quickly lead to higher gasoline prices
item.description: BISMARCK, N.D. (AP) — The nearly 2,700-mile Keystone oil pipeline was shut down Tuesday morning after it ruptured in North Dakota, halting the flow of millions of gallons of crude oil from Canada to refineries in the U.S. and potentially leading to higher gasoline prices.
item.dc:creator: JACK DURA and SARAH RAZA, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Repeat human smuggler sentenced in South Texas
item.description: A South Texas man who was arrested four times in the past year on human smuggling charges has been sentenced to prison, U.S. Attorney Nicholas Ganjei said Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: Sandra Sanchez, Fox 5

item.title: Rapper 'Fat Flow' gets 33 years for kidnapping migrants
item.description: A judge has sentenced a rapper calling himself Fat Flow to 33 years and four months in prison for the 2023 kidnapping of seven migrants in Juarez, Mexico.
item.dc:creator: Julian Resendiz, KTLA 5

item.title: Veterinarian goes missing after video showed him kicking horse
item.description: A Las Vegas veterinarian is missing following a viral video that shows him kicking a horse in the head. On Thursday, Dr. Shawn Frehner was paid to administer anesthetic shots for some horses at a Nevada property.
item.dc:creator: James Schaeffer, KTLA 5

item.title: Trump plans steep fines for immigrants who fail to leave after deportation order
item.description: The Trump administration is planning to fine migrants up to $998 a day if they fail to leave after being put under deportation orders, according to a report from Reuters.
item.dc:creator: Sophia Fanning, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Rising rivers threaten US South and Midwest after dayslong torrent of rain
item.description: FRANKFORT, Kentucky (AP) — Rivers rose and flooding worsened Sunday across the sodden U.S. South and Midwest, threatening communities already badly damaged by days of heavy rain and wind that killed at least 18 people.
item.dc:creator: JON CHERRY, KIMBERLEE KRUESI and ANTHONY IZAGUIRRE, Associated Press, Fox 5
